summary: Summary: The conversation begins with Speaker2 angry, confronting Speaker1 about Peter Burden and insisting Burden’s affection was real; Speaker1 responds frustrated and angry at being caught, then shifts to a more neutral defense. Speaker2 accuses Speaker1 of torturing them over a trivial brooch and expresses surprise at Speaker1’s nonchalance. The tone then moves to boredom and brandy: Speaker1 grows frustrated and angry at Speaker2’s increasing annoyance, while Speaker2 is briefly neutral (mentioning brandy) before turning frustrated and angry and accusing Speaker1 of being disagreeable. A dispute about “adders” follows, with Speaker1 frustrated and angry at the outcome and Speaker2 retorting with anger and frustration, prompting Speaker1’s “I don’t care” outburst. When Peter Burden is revisited, Speaker2 remains angry questioning past interactions, and Speaker1 is initially frustrated and angry but then settles into neutral recollection. Accusations of drunkenness escalate: Speaker1 becomes increasingly frustrated and angry, hurling insults and telling Speaker2 to shut up; Speaker2 stays combative, frustrated and angry. In a late-night disagreement over sound at a photographer’s studio, Speaker1’s frustration and anger intensify while Speaker2 refuses and admonishes Speaker1 with anger. The exchange culminates in mutual temperamental outbursts: both speakers unleash intense anger and frustration, exchange vicious insults (Speaker1 calling Speaker2 a “sadistic bully”), and then declare the relationship over—Speaker1 in anger and frustration declaring “the end,” and Speaker2 responding with anger and a vow never to see them again. Overall, Speaker2’s tone is dominated by anger with brief neutral moments, while Speaker1 moves from frustration into anger with intermittent neutrality before ending in anger and frustration.
